name: aligned_trivially_true
reason: If 1 is aligned to 2 the spec vacuously accepts non-multiples, making alignment meaningless.

name: aligned_zero_size
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_zero_size(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) holds for nonzero addr the spec fails to guard against division-by-zero undefined behavior.

name: aligned_successor
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_successor(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        s > 1,
        aligned(a, s),
    ensures
        aligned(a + 1, s),
{
}
reason: If a+1 is always aligned whenever a is, alignment collapses to a trivial property for every size greater than 1.

name: aligned_collapses_values
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_collapses_values(a: nat, b: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        s > 0,
        aligned(a, s),
        aligned(b, s),
    ensures
        a == b,
{
}
reason: Many distinct naturals are aligned to the same size; alignment must not force equality of all aligned addresses.

name: aligned_over_transitive
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_over_transitive(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        b > 0,
        c > 0,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(b, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, b * c),
{
}
reason: Transitivity gives aligned(a,c) not aligned(a,b*c); conflating the two would unsoundly strengthen alignment guarantees.
